core_mechanism: Localization/delocalization of perturbation error across a weakly- or sparsely-coupled high-dimensional dynamical system: a global discretization bias spreads over the coordinates so that any low-dimensional (K-variable) marginal accumulates only O(sqrt(K)) error, bounded by representing the discrete-time integrator's one-step propagator as a matrix polynomial whose off-diagonal decay is controlled by the coupling structure.
brief_reason: The abstract's central claim is that under weak or sparse interactions the bias 'delocalizes' so that controlling a K-dimensional marginal needs only O(sqrt K) steps, a result obtained via a matrix-polynomial characterization of the integrators' propagators.
